task_completion.description: Five distinct columns labeled 'Inputs', 'Activities', 'Outputs', 'Outcomes', and 'Impact' are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io canvas displays five sections or columns with the exact headers: Inputs, Activities, Outputs, Outcomes, and Impact.

task_completion.description: Items from the grant narrative are categorized into the columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text boxes under the headers contain specific details from the narrative (e.g., '$500,000', 'Python curriculum', '200 students', 'math scores', 'diverse workforce').

task_completion.description: Arrows show the flow from left to right.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Directed arrows connect the items or columns, pointing from left to right (e.g., from Inputs to Activities, Activities to Outputs, etc.).

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved as 'stem_logic_model.drawio' in the Diagrams folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io window title shows 'stem_logic_model.drawio', or a save dialog shows this filename in the 'Diagrams' directory.

task_completion.description: The diagram is exported as 'stem_logic_model.pdf' in the Diagrams folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog in draw.io, a PDF viewer showing the diagram, or a file manager showing 'stem_logic_model.pdf' in the 'Diagrams' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io graphical interface to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the draw.io application open with the diagram being actively edited on the canvas, not a text editor modifying XML.

integrity.description: The diagram contains authentic details from the provided narrative.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the diagram includes specific numbers and terms from the narrative (e.g., $500,000, 5,000 hours) rather than generic logic model examples.

integrity.description: The PDF was exported using the application's export feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the 'File -> Export as -> PDF' menu or the PDF export dialog in draw.io.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The grant narrative contains specific details that should appear in the diagram: Inputs ($500,000 TechFoundation grant, 12 staff, City Library), Activities (Python curriculum, bootcamps, hackathons), Outputs (200 students, 50 instructors, 5,000 hours), Outcomes (math scores, logic scores, confidence), and Impact (diverse workforce, reduce unemployment). The diagram must have exactly 5 columns: Inputs, Activities, Outputs, Outcomes, Impact.
